# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:14

# Update apt packages
RUN apt-get update -y

# Install build-essential for compiling source files
RUN apt-get install -y build-essential

# Install some common utilities
RUN apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl gnupg lsb-release

# Create .netrc file to avoid password prompts during npm install
RUN echo "machine registry.npmjs.org login default \
    password your_npm_token \
    "" > /root/.netrc

# Install Node.js dependencies
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install

# Copy the app source code to the container
COPY . .

# Expose port 3000 for production environment
EXPOSE 3000
